title: tetragonia galenioides
intro: Tetragonia galenioides, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōkihi, is a succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming Tetragonia galenioides has been linked to various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried or made into a tea, and can also be used in salads or as a cooked green, highlighting its versatility as a nutritious food source.
